data.description:
## Why
Existing stores must learn they are behind the current binary's ensure set without paying
hot-path cost. This is Rails `CheckPending` for a School-B registry: compare applied-set to
available-set, nudge on write activity — cheaply, rate-limited, never breaking a write.

## What
Add `.ensure-applied` (git-ignored, per-environment, atomically written) holding the SET of
cleanly-applied ensure ids (multi-actor store → set, not int; absent/garbage → empty set).
Hook the universal write chokepoint `event_append.write_and_push` (lazy import): compute
`pending = available_ids − applied_ids` ONCE per process, cache the boolean. Emit a WARNING
pointing to `rebar fsck --fix` only when pending AND `.ensure-hinted` timestamp > 24h old
(atomic update). Suppressible via env var. Tight exception swallow (marker read + timestamp
write only) + debug log — a write must never fail on the nag.

## Scope
In: `.ensure-applied` read/write, write-path hook, cached-once compute, 24h timestamp
rate-limit, suppress env var, fail-silent. Out: `run_ensures()` itself (WS1), fsck --fix (WS3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.ensure-applied` is atomic + concurrency-safe under parallel agents; absent/garbage degrades to empty-set (no crash)
- [ ] Write path computes pending once/process (cached boolean); a converged store adds ≤1 file read per process and nothing after
- [ ] Pure reads (show/list/search) are completely untouched — no marker read on the read path
- [ ] Hint points to `rebar fsck --fix`, is rate-limited to ≤1 per 24h per environment via a persisted timestamp (not per-process), and is suppressible via env var
- [ ] The hint path swallows its own exceptions (debug-logged) so a write never fails on it
- [ ] A fresh auto-init'd worktree (applied-set current from init) never hints
data.title: WS2: applied-set marker + write-path pending-hint (cached once/proc, 24h rate-limit, fail-silent)
